key_insight: The paper shows that phase-field fracture can be solved end-to-end in PyTorch without custom compiled code by using matrix-free tensor operations and differentiable implicit solves.
engineering_relevance: This makes large-scale fracture simulation portable across CPUs and GPUs while enabling gradient-based parameter identification and integration with optimization or machine-learning workflows.
method: Matrix-free finite-element mechanics using element-wise tensor contractions and scatter accumulation in PyTorch
Support for AT1/AT2 fracture regularizations, multiple energy splits, and plane strain/plane stress options
Autograd-compatible explicit dynamics plus a custom backward rule for the bound-constrained implicit damage solve via conjugate gradient
evidence_signal: The solver is benchmarked on four dynamic and two quasi-static fracture cases and demonstrated on meshes of about 10^6 nodes on a single NVIDIA A100 GPU, including recovery of fracture energy Gc with relative error below 10^-3.
watch_item: The implicit damage solve relies on a custom backward/CG-based differentiation path, so performance and accuracy may depend on solver settings and convergence behavior.
paper_title: A matrix-free, differentiable PyTorch solver for phase-field fracture: Formulation, benchmarks, and inverse analysis
